content: Health Technology Assessment (HTA) is a systematic evaluation of properties, effects, and impacts of health technologies (drugs, devices, procedures) to inform policy decisions. The UK's NICE (National Institute for Health and Care Excellence) pioneered using Quality-Adjusted Life Years (QALYs) to measure cost-effectiveness. A QALY combines quantity and quality of life, where 1 QALY equals one year in perfect health. NICE typically uses a threshold of £20,000-30,000 per QALY gained for recommending treatments. HTA considers clinical effectiveness, cost-effectiveness, ethical considerations, and impact on health inequalities.
